steps.title: Track the subject each one
steps.content: The subject is "each one," which is singular. The phrase "of the thousands of faux Chinese characters" and the long dash-enclosed modifier do not change the number of the subject.

steps.title: Use a singular present verb
steps.content: A is plural, so "each one holds no meaning" needs the singular form instead. C uses plural "have," and D uses plural "were" plus an awkward past-progressive form. The singular present-tense verb agrees with "each one." The answer is B.
